name: Broadcom Inc.
description: Broadcom Inc. is a global technology leader that designs, develops and supplies a broad range of semiconductor and infrastructure software solutions. The company's semiconductor solutions focus on complex digital and mixed-signal complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor based devices and analog III-V based products.
businessModel: Broadcom generates revenue through the sale of semiconductor devices for networking, broadband, enterprise storage, and industrial applications, as well as infrastructure software. In AI, it co-designs custom AI accelerators (ASICs) for hyperscalers like Google and Meta.
geopoliticalSummary: Broadcom's revenue is heavily concentrated in the Asia Pacific region, particularly China, which presents significant exposure to export controls and trade tensions. The company relies on a complex global supply chain with significant concentration risk in Taiwan for manufacturing.

revenueByGeography.note: Primary hub for semiconductor supply chain

keySuppliers.name: TSMC
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Sole source for advanced node manufacturing

regionalExposures.note: Significant revenue and supply chain exposure

infrastructureDependencies.name: Trans-Pacific Shipping
infrastructureDependencies.note: Transport of chips from Taiwan to US

aiComputeStack.what: Custom AI accelerators and networking chips

hbmRelationship.note: Uses HBM in ASICs from SK Hynix/Micron

customASICPartnerships.note: Lead partner for Google's AI training chips
Design partner for Meta's inference chips

vsNVIDIA.note: Custom ASICs offer better power efficiency for specific workloads vs NVIDIA GPUs

sources.name: Broadcom 10-K FY2024
sources.verbatimQuote: Our business, operations and financial results are subject to various risks and uncertainties, including those described below, that could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, cash flows, and the trading price of our common stock.

name: NVIDIA Corporation
description: NVIDIA Corporation is the pioneer of GPU-accelerated computing. The company specializes in markets where its computing platforms can provide tremendous acceleration for applications, including gaming, professional visualization, data center, and automotive.
businessModel: NVIDIA designs and sells GPUs for gaming and data centers, as well as networking solutions. Its AI revenue comes from selling high-performance GPUs (Hopper, Blackwell) used for AI training and inference in data centers.
geopoliticalSummary: NVIDIA faces significant geopolitical risks due to its dependence on TSMC in Taiwan for all advanced manufacturing and its exposure to U.S. export controls on sales to China.

revenueByGeography.note: Includes Hong Kong; impacted by export controls

keySuppliers.name: TSMC
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Sole source for advanced node GPUs and CoWoS packaging

commodityDependencies.note: Critical for AI GPUs, supplied by SK Hynix, Micron, Samsung

regionalExposures.note: Total dependency on Taiwan for manufacturing
Impact of US export controls on high-end GPU sales

hbmRelationship.note: Primary consumer of global HBM capacity

sources.name: NVIDIA 10-K FY2024
sources.verbatimQuote: In evaluating NVIDIA and our business, the following factors should be considered in addition to the other information in this Annual Report on Form 10-K.

name: Marvell Technology, Inc.
description: Marvell Technology, Inc. is a leader in infrastructure semiconductor solutions. The company provides a broad portfolio of data infrastructure semiconductor solutions spanning the data center core to the network edge.
businessModel: Marvell generates revenue by designing and selling semiconductor products for the data center, carrier infrastructure, enterprise networking, consumer and automotive markets. It focuses on custom AI ASICs and electro-optics (DSP).
geopoliticalSummary: Marvell has significant exposure to the Asia Pacific region for manufacturing and revenue. It relies on TSMC for its most advanced products and faces risks from U.S.-China trade restrictions.

regionalExposures.note: High dependence on Taiwan for manufacturing

aiComputeStack.what: Custom accelerators and PAM4 DSPs for AI interconnect

customASICPartnerships.note: Major partner for AWS AI training chips

vsNVIDIA.note: Provides connectivity for NVIDIA systems while building competing custom chips

competitiveMoat: Proven execution on complex custom ASICs
keyAIRisks: Loss of custom slots to competitors (Alchip/Broadcom)

sources.name: Marvell 10-K FY2024
sources.verbatimQuote: Investing in our common stock involves a high degree of risk.

name: Micron Technology, Inc.
description: Micron Technology, Inc. is a world leader in innovative memory and storage solutions. Through its global brands — Micron and Crucial — the company's broad portfolio of high-performance memory and storage technologies is transforming how the world uses information to enrich life.
businessModel: Micron manufactures DRAM and NAND memory products. AI revenue is driven by High Bandwidth Memory (HBM3e) and high-capacity DDR5 modules for AI servers.
geopoliticalSummary: Micron faces complex geopolitical risks, including China's CAC restrictions on its products and the competitive landscape with South Korean rivals. It is expanding U.S. manufacturing with CHIPS Act support.

revenueByGeography.note: Strong domestic growth driven by AI

regionalExposures.note: CAC security review limits sales to critical infrastructure

aiComputeStack.note: HBM is a critical bottleneck for AI hardware

hbmRelationship.note: Scaling HBM3e production for NVIDIA Blackwell

vsNVIDIA.note: Micron is a beneficiary of NVIDIA's growth

aiMarketShareSources.note: Aiming for share parity with DRAM

sources.name: Micron 10-K FY2024
sources.verbatimQuote: In addition to the factors discussed elsewhere in this Form 10-K, this section discusses important factors which could cause actual results or events to differ materially from those contained in any forward-looking statements made by us.

name: Super Micro Computer, Inc.
description: Super Micro Computer, Inc. is a global leader in high-performance, high-efficiency server technology and innovation. It provides advanced server, storage, networking, devices and software solutions optimized for diverse applications.
businessModel: SMCI sells server systems, subsystems, and accessories. It specializes in application-optimized servers, particularly for AI, utilizing liquid cooling and rapid time-to-market strategies.
geopoliticalSummary: SMCI manufactures primarily in the U.S., Taiwan, and Malaysia. It faces risks from component availability (GPUs) and potential export controls on high-end server systems.

keySuppliers.name: NVIDIA
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Primary source for AI server acceleration

aiComputeStack.what: AI server racks with liquid cooling

vsNVIDIA.note: SMCI is an essential channel for NVIDIA GPUs

sources.name: Super Micro 10-K FY2024
sources.verbatimQuote: Our business involves significant risks, some of which are described below.

name: Arm Holdings plc
description: Arm is the world's leading supplier of semiconductor intellectual property (IP). Arm designs the architecture that powers the majority of the world's chips, from smartphones to supercomputers.
businessModel: Arm generates revenue through licensing fees and royalties. In AI, its Armv9 architecture provides specialized instructions for AI workloads, and its Neoverse platform targets data center AI chips.
geopoliticalSummary: Arm's business in China is a major geopolitical risk, as it operates through Arm China, an entity it does not control. It also faces risks from UK and US export controls.

regionalExposures.note: Dependence on Arm China for revenue collection and IP enforcement

aiComputeStack.note: Deeply embedded in mobile and increasingly data center

vsNVIDIA.note: NVIDIA Grace CPU uses Arm architecture

sources.name: Arm 20-F FY2024
sources.verbatimQuote: In addition to the other information contained in this Annual Report and in other documents we file with or furnish to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the 'SEC'), the following risk factors should be considered in evaluating our business.

name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
description: TSMC is the world's largest dedicated semiconductor foundry. It manufactures chips for most of the world's leading semiconductor companies, including Apple, NVIDIA, and AMD.
businessModel: TSMC's revenue comes from manufacturing wafers for its customers. AI growth is driving demand for advanced nodes (3nm, 5nm) and CoWoS packaging.
geopoliticalSummary: TSMC is at the heart of the U.S.-China technology rivalry. Its concentration of manufacturing in Taiwan is a critical global chokepoint.

revenueByGeography.note: Dominated by US hyperscalers and fabless firms

keySuppliers.name: ASML
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Sole source for EUV lithography machines

regionalExposures.note: Cross-strait tensions pose an existential risk to operations

aiComputeStack.what: Advanced node wafers and CoWoS packaging
aiComputeStack.note: The ultimate chokepoint of the AI industry

hbmRelationship.note: Integrates HBM into customer chips via CoWoS

vsNVIDIA.note: TSMC is the essential partner for NVIDIA's existence

keyAIRisks: Energy and water shortages in Taiwan

sources.name: TSMC 20-F 2023
sources.verbatimQuote: The uncertainty and instability inherent in advanced technologies impose challenges for achieving expected product quality and product yield.

name: ASML Holding N.V.
description: ASML is the world's leading supplier to the semiconductor industry of photolithography systems. It is the sole manufacturer of Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines.
businessModel: ASML sells high-cost lithography systems and provides ongoing service and upgrades. EUV systems are required for all advanced AI chip manufacturing.
geopoliticalSummary: ASML is subject to Dutch and US export controls, particularly regarding its sales of advanced DUV and EUV tools to China.

revenueByGeography.note: Surge in DUV demand prior to restrictions
Driven by Samsung and SK Hynix

regionalExposures.note: US and Dutch government restrictions on tool exports

aiComputeStack.what: EUV and High-NA EUV lithography systems
aiComputeStack.note: Sole provider of technology needed for advanced nodes

keyAICustomers.name: TSMC
keyAICustomers.note: Purchases EUV systems for AI logic manufacturing

vsNVIDIA.note: NVIDIA chips cannot be made without ASML tools

keyAIRisks: Export control expansion to older DUV tools

sources.name: ASML 20-F 2023
sources.verbatimQuote: Many risks have the potential to impact our business and it is important to understand their nature.

name: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.
description: AMD is a global semiconductor company that designs and develops high-performance processors and graphics technologies for the data center, personal computer and gaming markets.
businessModel: AMD sells CPUs (EPYC) and GPUs (Instinct) for data centers, as well as PC and gaming hardware. AI revenue is driven by the Instinct MI300 series.
geopoliticalSummary: AMD relies on TSMC for manufacturing and faces risks from export controls on high-performance accelerators to China.

keySuppliers.name: TSMC
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Sole source for advanced node CPUs and GPUs

regionalExposures.note: Dependence on TSMC for all advanced products

hbmRelationship.note: Uses high quantities of HBM in Instinct GPUs

vsNVIDIA.note: Main challenger in AI training and inference

keyAIRisks: Allocation of HBM and CoWoS capacity

sources.name: AMD 10-K FY2023
sources.verbatimQuote: You should carefully consider the risks described below and elsewhere in this Annual Report on Form 10-K before making an investment decision.

name: Applied Materials, Inc.
description: Applied Materials is a leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. Its expertise in modifying materials at atomic levels and on an industrial scale enables customers to transform possibilities into reality.
businessModel: AMAT sells manufacturing equipment, services, and software to semiconductor, display, and related industries. It benefits from AI through increased demand for advanced logic and memory manufacturing tools.
geopoliticalSummary: AMAT has significant revenue exposure to China and is highly sensitive to export controls. It is a key player in the re-shoring of semiconductor manufacturing to the U.S. and Europe.

regionalExposures.note: Highly vulnerable to US government trade restrictions

aiComputeStack.what: Deposition, Etch, and Ion Implantation tools
aiComputeStack.note: Materials engineering is critical for advanced chip scaling

keyAICustomers.name: TSMC
keyAICustomers.note: Uses AMAT tools for advanced logic

vsNVIDIA.note: AMAT tools are used to build NVIDIA GPUs

competitiveMoat: Broadest tool portfolio in the industry
Large installed base and service revenue
keyAIRisks: China export controls on advanced tools

sources.name: Applied Materials 10-K FY2024
sources.verbatimQuote: The following risk factors could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition or results of operations and cause reputational harm, and should be carefully considered in evaluating our business, in addition to other information presented elsewhere in this report.
